# Test-Quality Review — `lib/features/agents/tools/` + `test/features/agents/tools/`

**Scope:** All 13 implementation files under `lib/features/agents/tools/` and their 13 mirrored test files under `test/features/agents/tools/`.

*Generated by automated test-quality review (static analysis only)*

---

## Per-file table

| File (impl) | Lines | Has test? | Top issue |
|---|---|---|---|
| `agent_tool_registry.dart` | 904 | Yes (567 lines) | **[HIGH] OVERSIZED** — 904 lines, pure data (const definitions + set/map constants); only 567-line test covers ≈18 per-tool groups but leaves `enabled` field and full `deferredTools`/`explodedBatchTools` membership untested |
| `agent_tool_executor.dart` | 417 | Yes (697 lines) | Good coverage; `fromHandlerResult` assert (`didWrite=true` with null `entityId`) path untested |
| `checklist_migration_handler.dart` | 182 | Yes (1407 lines) | **[HIGH] OVERSIZED TEST** — 1407-line test dwarfs 182-line impl; extensive but very repetitive setup blocks |
| `correction_examples_builder.dart` | 72 | Yes (373 lines) | Test quality good; Glados present; `buildContext` DB-exception path only exercises "returns empty" but doesn't assert the exception is swallowed silently (minor) |
| `follow_up_task_handler.dart` | 304 | Yes (1379 lines) | **[HIGH] OVERSIZED TEST** — 1379 vs 304 lines; no Glados for `_parsePriority`/`_parseDueDate`/`_daysInMonth` pure-logic paths even though date+priority parsing is generative-test-worthy |
| `project_tool_definitions.dart` | 213 | Yes (300 lines) | Registry-schema tests only; `AgentToolDefinition.enabled` field never asserted; `projectDeferredTools` membership not tested |
| `running_timer_update_handler.dart` | 157 | Yes (683 lines) | **[HIGH] OVERSIZED TEST** — 683 vs 157 lines; Glados present; `updateCurrent` side-effect (in-memory update after persistence) not explicitly asserted |
| `task_label_handler.dart` | 210 | Yes (1027 lines) | **[HIGH] OVERSIZED TEST** — 1027 vs 210 lines; Glados present; `buildLabelContext` category-scope filtering logic (global vs. in-category) could use more precise assertions |
| `task_language_handler.dart` | 162 | Yes (562 lines) | Glados present; inline `registerFallbackValue` instead of centralized `registerAllFallbackValues` |
| `task_status_handler.dart` | 238 | Yes (801 lines) | Glados present; `_buildStatus` with `BLOCKED`/`ON HOLD` `reason` field value propagation not verified via Glados across all reason shapes |
| `task_title_handler.dart` | 207 | Yes (474 lines) | Glados present; `fromHandlerResult` assert (`didWrite=true, entityId=null`) not tested — will only surface in debug builds |
| `time_entry_handler.dart` | 240 | Yes (878 lines) | **[MED] OVERSIZED TEST** — 878 vs 240 lines; **no Glados** for ISO-8601 parsing / `_isSameDay` boundary logic despite clear generative opportunity |
| `time_entry_update_handler.dart` | 214 | Yes (771 lines) | Glados present; `_formatUpdatedRange` output format not directly asserted in property tests |

---

## File size / split opportunities

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `lib/features/agents/tools/agent_tool_registry.dart` — 904 lines. This is **the only file ≥ 1 000-line threshold** and crosses the soft 500-line advisory. It is almost entirely const data (tool definitions for task-agent, evolution-agent, and soul-evolution-agent, plus name-constant classes and set/map constants). Suggested split:
  - `agent_tool_registry.dart` — keep `AgentToolRegistry` class, `TaskAgentToolNames`, `deferredTools`, `explodedBatchTools`, `taskAgentTools` (≈ 750 lines of the current file).
  - `evolution_tool_registry.dart` — `EvolutionToolNames`, `evolutionAgentTools`, `soulEvolutionAgentTools`.
  - The test file at `test/features/agents/tools/agent_tool_registry_test.dart` should mirror the split.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/agents/tools/checklist_migration_handler_test.dart` — 1 407 lines for a 182-line impl. Extract a `_TestBench` class holding mock setup, factory helpers (`makeChecklistItem`, `makeTask`), and `_generatedMetadata` utilities; this eliminates ≈200 lines of repeated boilerplate across the Glados and static test groups. **RESOLVED:** five shared stub helpers (`stubItemLookup`/`stubSourceTask`/`stubTargetTask`/`stubArchiveUpdate`/`stubAddItemToChecklist`) replace ~25 repeated stub blocks; the `makeChecklistItem`/`makeTask` factories already existed (1407 → 1273 lines).

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/agents/tools/follow_up_task_handler_test.dart` — 1 379 lines for a 304-line impl. The extensive static test bodies each spend 30–50 lines on identical mock-stub blocks. Extract `_stubSourceTask()`, `_stubNewTask()`, `_stubLinkCreation()` helper functions; consider a `_TestBench` class for the `handlerWithProject` variant. **RESOLVED:** `stubSourceTaskLookup`/`stubCreateTask`/`stubLinkCreation` helpers replace 58 verbatim stub blocks across the static groups.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/agents/tools/task_label_handler_test.dart` — 1 027 lines for a 210-line impl. Inline Glados scenario objects and the `buildLabelContext` static-test group both repeat `MockLabelAssignmentProcessor` setup. Extract a `_stubProcessor()` helper.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/agents/tools/running_timer_update_handler_test.dart` — 683 lines for a 157-line impl. Static tests individually stub `_timeService.getCurrent()` and `_timeService.linkedFrom` in every test body; extract `_stubActiveTimer()` / `_stubNoTimer()` helpers.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/features/agents/tools/time_entry_handler_test.dart` — 878 lines for a 240-line impl. Multiple test bodies repeat `createMetadata`+`createDbEntity` stub blocks; extract `_stubSuccessfulCreation(tester, {DateTime? endTime})`.

---

## Test quality improvements

- [x] **[HIGH]** `agent_tool_registry_test.dart` — `AgentToolDefinition.enabled` field is never asserted. `get_related_task_details` is the only tool with `enabled: false`; add a test: `expect(tool.enabled, isFalse)`. All other tools should assert `enabled: true` (or at minimum the default value of `true`). This field is load-bearing for LLM tool exposure.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `agent_tool_registry_test.dart` — `deferredTools` membership is only spot-checked for `createTimeEntry` and `updateTimeEntry`. Add a systematic test verifying the full expected set of 14 deferred tool names and that non-deferred tools (e.g. `updateReport`, `recordObservations`) are absent from the set.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `agent_tool_registry_test.dart` — `explodedBatchTools` is tested only for `assign_task_labels`. Add assertions for all 4 entries: `addMultipleChecklistItems→'items'`, `updateChecklistItems→'items'`, `assignTaskLabels→'labels'`, `migrateChecklistItems→'items'`.

- [ ] **[MED]** `agent_tool_executor_test.dart` — The `ToolExecutionResult.fromHandlerResult` factory contains an `assert` (`lib/features/agents/tools/agent_tool_executor.dart:41–44`) that fires when `didWrite=true` and `entityId` is null or empty. This is never triggered by any test. Add a debug-mode assertion test, or at minimum document the gap. In release builds this is silently skipped.

- [ ] **[MED]** `running_timer_update_handler_test.dart` — The `TimeService.updateCurrent(...)` side-effect call (line 134–141 in impl) is tested by the Glados property that checks `persistenceSucceeds` flag, but the test never calls `verify(() => mockTimeService.updateCurrent(captureAny())).called(1)` to assert the updated entity's text and `dateTo` fields. The in-memory sync is load-bearing for the next-wake context.

- [ ] **[MED]** `task_language_handler_test.dart`, `task_title_handler_test.dart`, `task_status_handler_test.dart`, `task_label_handler_test.dart` — All four files use inline `registerFallbackValue(task as JournalEntity)` in `setUp()` instead of calling `registerAllFallbackValues()` from `test/helpers/fallbacks.dart`. This diverges from the centralized-fallbacks rule and can cause `MissingStubError` if new fallback types are introduced.

- [ ] **[MED]** `project_tool_definitions_test.dart` — `projectDeferredTools` (the `const Set<String>` listing deferred project tools) has no test at all. Add: verify it contains exactly `recommendNextSteps`, `updateProjectStatus`, `createTask`.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `correction_examples_builder_test.dart` — `buildContext` is tested for "returns empty when category is null" and "returns empty when category has no examples", but the `catch (_) { return ''; }` branch (when `getCategoryById` throws) has no explicit test. Add a test that stubs `mockJournalDb.getCategoryById` to throw and asserts the result is `''` (not a rethrown exception).

- [ ] **[LOW]** `time_entry_update_handler_test.dart` — `_formatUpdatedRange` is a private method called only via the `handle` success path. The Glados property checks `output.contains('Updated')` but never pins the `HH:mm–HH:mm` format shape. Add an assertion on `result.output` that matches the exact format regex `r'\d{2}:\d{2}–\d{2}:\d{2}'` in at least one static success test.

---

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `follow_up_task_handler_test.dart` — `FollowUpTaskHandler._parsePriority` and `_parseDueDate` are non-trivial pure static methods. `_parsePriority` has a subtle fallback-detection pattern (calls `taskPriorityFromString` then re-checks case-normalized input against the known `{'P0','P1','P2','P3'}` set). `_parseDueDate` validates month (1–12) and day (1–N) ranges via regex + `_daysInMonth`. These are currently tested only by a handful of example-based cases in the Glados `followUpDueDateScenario` generator for `_parseDueDate`, but `_parsePriority` has no Glados property at all. Add a `Glados` property for `_parsePriority` covering the priority-string space (valid/invalid/padded/non-string), tagged `glados`. **RESOLVED:** `parsePriority` exposed `@visibleForTesting`; new Glados property (160 runs) covers case/padding variants over the P0–P3 space plus junk strings, with explicit null-default and non-string rejection tests.

- [ ] **[MED]** `time_entry_handler_test.dart` — `TimeEntryHandler` uses `parseTimeEntryLocalDateTime` (from `features/agents/time_entry_datetime.dart`) and `_isSameDay` for boundary-sensitive temporal validation. The tests are entirely example-based (no Glados). The `_isSameDay` check (same year/month/day in local time) is a pure function with clear algebraic properties (reflexive, symmetric over same-day pairs, asymmetric for cross-day pairs). A Glados property over `(DateTime a, DateTime b)` pairs would catch off-by-one bugs. Note: `time_entry_datetime_test.dart` already exists in the parent `agents/` directory and has Glados; verify the impl methods tested there before adding new properties, to avoid duplication.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `agent_tool_registry_test.dart` — `AgentToolRegistry.taskAgentTools` is a const list of 18 data objects. A Glados property is not appropriate here (it is fixture data, not parametric logic). **No action needed.**

- [ ] **[LOW]** `agent_tool_executor_test.dart` — `AgentToolExecutor.execute` is async and depends on I/O callbacks; Glados is a poor fit. The existing example-based tests are appropriate. **No action needed.**

---

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [x] **[HIGH]** `agent_tool_registry_test.dart` — The `retractSuggestions` tool (line 550–598 in impl) is defined in `taskAgentTools` but has no dedicated per-tool test group in the registry test. The schema has nested `proposals[].fingerprint` and `proposals[].reason` with `minItems:1` and `minLength:1`/`maxLength:500` constraints that no test currently touches.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `agent_tool_registry_test.dart` — The `createFollowUpTask` and `migrateChecklistItems` tools have no dedicated per-tool groups. `migrateChecklistItems` requires both `items` and `targetTaskId`, and `items[].id`/`items[].title` are both required — none of this is tested.

- [ ] **[MED]** `agent_tool_executor_test.dart` — The `_recordMessage` helper (`lib/features/agents/tools/agent_tool_executor.dart:382–416`) creates either 1 or 2 `upsertEntity` calls depending on whether `payloadText` is `null`. The null-payload path (called when `payloadText == null`) is never explicitly exercised. Add a test that stubs a handler returning a result with `output: ''` and verifies that only 1 entity (the message, no payload) is upserted for that leg.

- [ ] **[MED]** `time_entry_handler_test.dart` — The `timer start failed after persistence` path (`lib/features/agents/tools/time_entry_handler.dart:205–213`) — where `createDbEntity` succeeds but `_timeService.start()` throws — is not covered. This code path returns `success: false` but with a non-null `mutatedEntityId`. Add a test: stub `createDbEntity` to return `true` and `_timeService.start` to throw; assert `result.success == false` and `result.mutatedEntityId` is the created entry's ID.

- [ ] **[MED]** `time_entry_update_handler_test.dart` — The check that `entry is not a JournalEntry` (line 88–94 in impl) is only exercised via the Glados scenario's `isActiveTimer` flag, not for a non-`JournalEntry` entity type (e.g., a `Task` or `ChecklistItem`). Add an explicit static test where `journalDb.journalEntityById` returns a `Task` and assert `result.errorMessage == 'Unsupported entry type'`.

- [ ] **[MED]** `task_status_handler_test.dart` — `TaskStatusHandler._buildStatus` is called for every allowed status. The `BLOCKED` and `ON HOLD` branches pass `reason ?? 'No reason provided'` to the `TaskStatus` constructor. No test verifies that the `reason` field lands on the persisted `TaskStatus` object. Add a test that asserts the captured `updatedTask.data.status` carries the trimmed reason string for a BLOCKED transition.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `running_timer_update_handler_test.dart` — `RunningTimerUpdateFailure` constants class (lines 9–16 in impl) is not directly asserted in any test. The Glados property implicitly exercises the values via `errorMessage` string matching, but adding one assertion per constant (e.g. `expect(result.errorMessage, RunningTimerUpdateFailure.invalidSummary)`) would future-proof the string values.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `project_tool_definitions_test.dart` — The `recordObservations` tool in `projectAgentTools` uses `oneOf` in its `items` schema (line 88 in impl) — a schema construct not used anywhere else in either agent tool set. No test verifies the `oneOf` structure. Add a test that checks `itemSchema['oneOf']` is a `List` with two elements (string schema and object schema).

---

## Test execution speed opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/agents/tools/checklist_migration_handler_test.dart:803` — Glados property at `numRuns: 220`. The test file (1 407 lines) has heavy repeated mock-stub setup throughout; the Glados iteration benefits from the same `setUp`-to-`setUpAll` optimization as other files — mock objects and their stubs rebuilt 220 times. Extracting shared stub helpers (`_stubTaskRepo`, `_stubChecklistItems`) and constructing the mock once per suite via `setUpAll` would cut per-iteration overhead. **RESOLVED (partially assessed):** the shared stub helpers landed (see the _TestBench item above). The setUpAll/mock-reuse half is not applicable — the Glados body verifies call counts per iteration, so fresh mocks (or an equivalent-cost reset) per scenario are required for sound verification.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/agents/tools/running_timer_update_handler_test.dart:431` — Glados property at `numRuns: 220`. The quality section already notes that `_stubActiveTimer()` / `_stubNoTimer()` helpers are missing; their absence means each of the 220 Glados iterations rebuilds identical `when(mockTimeService.getCurrent())` stubs inline. Extracting these helpers reduces per-iteration cost and is a combined speed + DRY win. **RESOLVED:** `stubActiveTimer({timer, linkedFrom})` / `stubNoTimer()` helpers replace the inline getCurrent/linkedFrom stub pairs across the static tests; the Glados body keeps its scenario-driven local stubs.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/agents/tools/time_entry_update_handler_test.dart:512` — Glados property at `numRuns: 240` (highest in the tools directory). The `setUp` block rebuilds `MockJournalDb`, `MockTimeService`, and all their stubs on every Glados iteration. Moving the construction to `setUpAll` with targeted per-test reset would eliminate ~230 redundant stub registrations.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/agents/tools/follow_up_task_handler_test.dart:345` — Glados property at `numRuns: 180`. Same pattern: heavy per-`setUp` mock wiring. The `_stubSourceTask()` / `_stubNewTask()` helper extraction recommended in the size section would also reduce per-iteration setup time.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/agents/tools/task_label_handler_test.dart:607`, `task_language_handler_test.dart:395`, `task_status_handler_test.dart:585,662`, `task_title_handler_test.dart:314` — Glados properties at `numRuns: 180`, `180`, `180/120`, and `180` respectively. Each of these files rebuilds mock objects in `setUp`. Collectively the five files run ~940 mock-rebuild iterations; the shared `setUpAll` + `reset` pattern would reduce this to ~5 constructions.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/features/agents/tools/correction_examples_builder_test.dart:363` — Glados property at `numRuns: 140`. The service under test has a single `MockJournalDb` stub; construction is cheap. Low impact.

---

## Summary

- **1 oversized impl file**: `agent_tool_registry.dart` at 904 lines — split into task-agent and evolution-agent sub-files recommended.
- **5 oversized test files**: `checklist_migration_handler_test.dart` (1 407), `follow_up_task_handler_test.dart` (1 379), `task_label_handler_test.dart` (1 027), `running_timer_update_handler_test.dart` (683), `time_entry_handler_test.dart` (878) — all need `_TestBench` or stub-helper extraction.
- **4 test-quality issues at HIGH priority**: `enabled` field never asserted in registry tests; `deferredTools` membership only spot-checked; `explodedBatchTools` only 1-of-4 entries verified; inline `registerFallbackValue` in 4 files instead of centralized helper.
- **2 genuine Glados gaps**: `_parsePriority` in `follow_up_task_handler` has no property test; `TimeEntryHandler`'s `_isSameDay` has no property test (though `time_entry_datetime_test.dart` should be checked for overlap first).
- **Biggest single opportunity**: `agent_tool_registry_test.dart` — the `retractSuggestions`, `createFollowUpTask`, and `migrateChecklistItems` tools have no per-tool test groups despite schema constraints (`minItems`, `minLength`, dual required fields) that need explicit verification; and the `enabled` / full `deferredTools` membership gaps mean the LLM exposure logic is under-tested.
